channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sahur: Word of the Day for July 5, 2026
item.description: The article highlights the Word of the Day for July 5, 2026, which is 'sahur.' This term refers to the early morning meal traditionally consumed by Muslims during Ramadan, marking the beginning of the...

item.title: Anambra hairstylist recounts losing leg in accident and facing heartbreak
item.description: A 22-year-old Anambra hairstylist, who had recently been accepted into university, lost her leg in a tragic accident caused by a speeding bus. The incident occurred at the Oraifite Police Station, whe...

item.title: Pride in London 2026: highlights from the annual LGBTQ+ parade in pictures
item.description: This photo feature captures scenes from the annual Pride in London parade, held in the city centre and attended by members and supporters of the LGBTQ+ community. The gallery showcases a vibrant publi...

item.title: BC Nurses' Union to Picket Vancouver General Hospital Amid Strike Dispute
item.description: The BC Nurses’ Union has announced plans to stage provincewide strikes and pickets at Vancouver General Hospital starting next week, escalating tensions over working conditions and healthcare system d...

item.title: Faith Kipyegon and Faith Cherotich's Earnings from Eugene Diamond League Revealed
item.description: Faith Kipyegon and Faith Cherotich competed in the 2026 Eugene Diamond League, with Kipyegon finishing third in the women's mile and Cherotich winning the women's steeplechase. The event, held in Oreg...

item.title: Zanzibar and Mixx launch digital reforms for cooperative sectors
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (RG) has partnered with Mixx by Yas to advance digital transformation in cooperative societies, enhancing transparency, accountability, and financial inclusion...

item.title: AI's Limitations in Literary Translation: The Challenge of Poetry
item.description: The article explores how artificial intelligence struggles with translating poetry due to its reliance on metaphor, complex sentence structures, and emotional depth. Using the example of Urdu poet Fai...

item.title: Trump Addresses Nation on America's 250th Anniversary at Salute to America Celebration
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to deliver a 'historic' speech Saturday night at the Salute to America celebration on the National Mall, marking America's 250th anniversary. The event, which includes a ...

item.title: Springboks dominate England in Johannesburg rugby clash despite ticket price concerns
item.description: South Africa's Springboks delivered a commanding 45-21 victory over England in a rugby Nations Championship match at Ellis Park Stadium in Johannesburg, despite initial concerns about ticket prices. T...

item.title: Kadjebi District Distributes Treated Mosquito Nets to 4,039 Students
item.description: In a school-based malaria prevention initiative, 4,039 learners from 76 basic schools in Kadjebi District, Oti Region, received insecticide-treated mosquito nets. The distribution, organized by PMI Ve...

item.title: Coles Wanniassa Supermarket to Remain Open Despite Rumors
item.description: A local community in Wanniassa expressed concern over rumors circulating about the potential closure of the Coles supermarket, sparking a wave of support from residents. The initial post on the Kambah...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarterfinals as France Prepares for Heat Challenge
item.description: Morocco secured their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a 3-0 victory over Canada, ending the co-hosts' tournament hopes. The Atlas Lions' triumph, marked by goals from Azzedine Ounahi and So...

item.title: Springbok Coach Rassie Erasmus Reflects on Victory Over England Amid Injury Concerns
item.description: Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus expressed satisfaction with the team's 45-21 victory over England at Ellis Park, though he highlighted the significant injury toll. Key players like Ox Nche suffered a s...

item.title: Heat warnings persist in Sudbury as summer temperatures rise
item.description: Greater Sudbury, Ontario, is experiencing an extended heat warning due to persistent hot and humid weather conditions. The situation poses significant challenges for outdoor workers, with temperatures...

item.title: Chhattisgarh Cabinet Focuses on AI, Rural Economy, and Governance Enhancements
item.description: The Chhattisgarh cabinet convened its third 'Chintan Shivir' training session at IIM Raipur, emphasizing artificial intelligence, rural economic development, and modern governance. Chief Minister Vish...

item.title: Morocco Advances to World Cup Quarters with 3-0 Win Against Canada
item.description: Morocco delivered a clinical 3-0 victory over Canada in the FIFA World Cup, marking the first co-host exit from the tournament. The match, held in Houston, saw Morocco's midfielders Azzedine Ounahi an...

item.title: Rediscovered Kodachrome Slides Capture the Vibrant Spirit of the 1957 Rose Parade in Pasadena
item.description: The 1957 Rose Parade in Pasadena, California, marked a vibrant celebration of community, culture, and tradition on New Year’s Day. Rediscovered Kodachrome slides from this event offer a vivid glimpse ...

item.title: Sound Wave Technology Revolutionizes Kidney Stone Treatment in China
item.description: Medical professionals in China are increasingly using sound wave technology to treat kidney stones, offering a non-invasive alternative to traditional surgery. Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy us...

item.title: All Blacks Edge Out France in High-Stakes Nations Championship Debut
item.description: The All Blacks delivered a hard-fought victory against France in the first-ever Nations Championship Test, showcasing their resilience and tactical discipline. Despite facing challenges, the team's sc...

item.title: 52 Students from Corona School Excel Academically and Extracurricularly
item.description: The Corona School Trust Council celebrated the graduation of 52 students from its Agbara Campus in Ogun State, highlighting their academic excellence and diverse achievements. Principal Innocent Oaikh...
